decisions.title: Prioritize ROM (Rules Orchestration Manager) for Q2 delivery
decisions.context: ROM prototype (ENG-9250) and spec (ENG-9238) are in flight. This is critical for 'Build the Moat' pillar - enabling sophisticated multi-step decision logic that competitors can't easily replicate. Delays could impact customer commitments.

arguments.for: ROM is a differentiator for complex fraud detection workflows. Customers are asking for more sophisticated rule chaining. Aligns with 'Embed Deeper' by enabling more complex use cases.
arguments.against: Team capacity is strained with Sift Edge metrics work (ENG-10242-10246). May need to delay Edge observability improvements or pull engineers from Canvas features.

decisions.title: Accelerate Sift Edge production readiness vs. new feature development
decisions.context: Multiple Edge metrics stories (ENG-9561, 10243, 10244, 10246, 10242) indicate push for production monitoring. Edge is key to 'Nail the Core' (reliability) but competes with feature velocity. CVE-2026-33186 adds urgency.
decisions.blockedBy: Resource allocation between observability and feature work unclear. Critical CVE needs immediate resolution before production push.

arguments.for: Edge reliability directly impacts North Star metric - unreliable ingestion means no decisions captured. Production-grade monitoring is prerequisite for scaling. CVE is critical security risk.
arguments.against: Customers are waiting for Canvas v2 features (version history ENG-9442, CEL autocomplete ENG-9538). Delaying customer-facing features could hurt adoption metrics.

decisions.title: Consolidate identity/auth work (IdP, ABAC, SSO) into single initiative
decisions.context: Three separate auth stories (ENG-8081, 8157, 9761) suggest fragmented approach to enterprise auth requirements. 'Embed Deeper' requires robust enterprise controls. Coordination could accelerate delivery.

arguments.for: Enterprise customers need complete auth story, not piecemeal features. Single initiative could ship comprehensive solution faster. Critical for enterprise expansion (Embed Deeper pillar).
arguments.against: Teams may already be in flight on separate tracks. Consolidation creates coordination overhead. Different customers have different auth priorities.

decisions.title: Address Python Rules technical debt vs. Canvas feature velocity
decisions.context: Backend errors (ENG-9898 panic handling, ENG-10049 sigma bounds bug, ENG-10298 histogram bug) indicate quality issues. Canvas features (ENG-9442, 9538, 10086) are progressing but may be built on unstable foundation.

arguments.for: Bugs in core calculation engine undermine North Star metric - bad decisions are worse than no decisions. Panic handling (ENG-9898) is production stability risk. Nail the Core requires quality.
arguments.against: Canvas UX improvements drive adoption. Customers see UI improvements immediately. Backend fixes are invisible. Market pressure requires visible progress.

decisions.title: Scale assessment: Prioritize compaction performance (ENG-10237) before next enterprise deployment
decisions.context: Gov-scale compaction assessment in progress. Likely testing ground for Embed Deeper enterprise expansion. Performance issues at scale could block major deals.

arguments.for: Enterprise deals require proven scale. Performance issues discovered post-sale are costly. Assessment should drive architecture decisions now. Critical for 'Embed Deeper' in regulated industries.
arguments.against: May reveal need for significant re-architecture, delaying other work. Current customers operating at lower scale don't require this yet.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy coordination week: 3 architecture reviews (ROM, Edge metrics, Auth consolidation), sprint planning, 2 customer escalations (compaction performance, ingestion reliability). Free time concentrated Wed AM, Fri PM.
alerts: Critical CVE-2026-33186 response meeting added Thu 10am - displaces Edge architecture review
Compaction assessment readout scheduled Fri but results not ready - reschedule or present partial?
ROM spec review has 8 attendees - consider async review to reclaim time
Back-to-back 1:1s Tue PM (3 hrs) - no buffer for overruns or breaks
